speaker: HUBERT
text: Heat me these irons hot, and look thou stand

speaker: HUBERT
text: Within the arras. When I strike my foot

speaker: HUBERT
text: Upon the bosom of the ground, rush forth

speaker: HUBERT
text: And bind the boy which you shall find with me

speaker: HUBERT
text: Fast to the chair. Be heedful. Hence, and watch.

speaker: EXECUTIONER
text: I hope your warrant will bear out the deed.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Uncleanly scruples fear not you. Look to ’t.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Young lad, come forth. I have to say with you.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Good morrow, Hubert.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Good morrow, little prince.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: As little prince, having so great a title

speaker: ARTHUR
text: To be more prince, as may be. You are sad.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Indeed, I have been merrier.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Mercy on me!

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Methinks nobody should be sad but I.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Yet I remember, when I was in France,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Young gentlemen would be as sad as night

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Only for wantonness. By my christendom,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: So I were out of prison and kept sheep,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I should be as merry as the day is long.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And so I would be here but that I doubt

speaker: ARTHUR
text: My uncle practices more harm to me.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: He is afraid of me, and I of him.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Is it my fault that I was Geoffrey’s son?

speaker: ARTHUR
text: No, indeed, is ’t not. And I would to heaven

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I were your son, so you would love me, Hubert.

speaker: HUBERT
text: If I talk to him, with his innocent prate

speaker: HUBERT
text: He will awake my mercy, which lies dead.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Therefore I will be sudden and dispatch.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Are you sick, Hubert? You look pale today.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: In sooth, I would you were a little sick

speaker: ARTHUR
text: That I might sit all night and watch with you.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I warrant I love you more than you do me.

speaker: HUBERT
text: His words do take possession of my bosom.

speaker: HUBERT
text:
Read here, young Arthur.
                 
                (
                Aside
                .
                )
               How now, foolish rheum?

speaker: HUBERT
text: Turning dispiteous torture out of door?

speaker: HUBERT
text: I must be brief lest resolution drop

speaker: HUBERT
text: Out at mine eyes in tender womanish tears.—

speaker: HUBERT
text: Can you not read it? Is it not fair writ?

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Too fairly, Hubert, for so foul effect.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Must you with hot irons burn out both mine eyes?

speaker: HUBERT
text: Young boy, I must.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And will you?

speaker: HUBERT
text: And I will.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Have you the heart? When your head did but ache,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I knit my handkercher about your brows—

speaker: ARTHUR
text: The best I had, a princess wrought it me—

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And I did never ask it you again;

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And with my hand at midnight held your head,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And like the watchful minutes to the hour

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Still and anon cheered up the heavy time,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Saying “What lack you?” and “Where lies your grief?”

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Or “What good love may I perform for you?”

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Many a poor man’s son would have lien still

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And ne’er have spoke a loving word to you;

speaker: ARTHUR
text: But you at your sick service had a prince.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Nay, you may think my love was crafty love,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And call it cunning. Do, an if you will.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: If heaven be pleased that you must use me ill,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Why then you must. Will you put out mine eyes—

speaker: ARTHUR
text: These eyes that never did nor never shall

speaker: ARTHUR
text: So much as frown on you?

speaker: HUBERT
text: I have sworn to do it.

speaker: HUBERT
text: And with hot irons must I burn them out.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Ah, none but in this Iron Age would do it.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: The iron of itself, though heat red-hot,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Approaching near these eyes, would drink my tears

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And quench this fiery indignation

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Even in the matter of mine innocence;

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Nay, after that, consume away in rust

speaker: ARTHUR
text: But for containing fire to harm mine eye.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Are you more stubborn-hard than hammered iron?

speaker: ARTHUR
text: An if an angel should have come to me

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And told me Hubert should put out mine eyes,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I would not have believed him. No tongue but Hubert’s.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Come forth.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Do as I bid you do.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: O, save me, Hubert, save me! My eyes are out

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Even with the fierce looks of these bloody men.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Give me the iron, I say, and bind him here.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Alas, what need you be so boist’rous-rough?

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I will not struggle; I will stand stone-still.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: For God’s sake, Hubert, let me not be bound!

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Nay, hear me, Hubert! Drive these men away,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And I will sit as quiet as a lamb.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I will not stir nor wince nor speak a word

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Nor look upon the iron angerly.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Thrust but these men away, and I’ll forgive you,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Whatever torment you do put me to.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Go stand within. Let me alone with him.

speaker: EXECUTIONER
text: I am best pleased to be from such a deed.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Alas, I then have chid away my friend!

speaker: ARTHUR
text: He hath a stern look but a gentle heart.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Let him come back, that his compassion may

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Give life to yours.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Come, boy, prepare yourself.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Is there no remedy?

speaker: HUBERT
text: None but to lose your eyes.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: O God, that there were but a mote in yours,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: A grain, a dust, a gnat, a wandering hair,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Any annoyance in that precious sense.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Then, feeling what small things are boisterous there,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Your vile intent must needs seem horrible.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Is this your promise? Go to, hold your tongue.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Hubert, the utterance of a brace of tongues

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Must needs want pleading for a pair of eyes.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Let me not hold my tongue. Let me not, Hubert,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Or, Hubert, if you will, cut out my tongue,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: So I may keep mine eyes. O, spare mine eyes,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Though to no use but still to look on you.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Lo, by my troth, the instrument is cold,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And would not harm me.

speaker: HUBERT
text: I can heat it, boy.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: No, in good sooth. The fire is dead with grief,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Being create for comfort, to be used

speaker: ARTHUR
text: In undeserved extremes. See else yourself.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: There is no malice in this burning coal.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: The breath of heaven hath blown his spirit out

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And strewed repentant ashes on his head.

speaker: HUBERT
text: But with my breath I can revive it, boy.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: An if you do, you will but make it blush

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And glow with shame of your proceedings, Hubert.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Nay, it perchance will sparkle in your eyes,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: And, like a dog that is compelled to fight,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Snatch at his master that doth tar him on.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: All things that you should use to do me wrong

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Deny their office. Only you do lack

speaker: ARTHUR
text: That mercy which fierce fire and iron extends,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Creatures of note for mercy-lacking uses.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Well, see to live. I will not touch thine eye

speaker: HUBERT
text: For all the treasure that thine uncle owes.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Yet am I sworn, and I did purpose, boy,

speaker: HUBERT
text: With this same very iron to burn them out.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: O, now you look like Hubert. All this while

speaker: ARTHUR
text: You were disguisèd.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Peace. No more. Adieu.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Your uncle must not know but you are dead.

speaker: HUBERT
text: I’ll fill these doggèd spies with false reports.

speaker: HUBERT
text: And, pretty child, sleep doubtless and secure

speaker: HUBERT
text: That Hubert, for the wealth of all the world,

speaker: HUBERT
text: Will not offend thee.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: O heaven! I thank you, Hubert.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Silence. No more. Go closely in with me.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Much danger do I undergo for thee.
